introSection: Jungle Rush is a steel roller coaster at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia. It was built by Vekoma as the world's first Switchback rollercoaster. Jungle rush opened to the public on December 23, 2024.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Jungle Rush features an inclined turntable, which allows the train to change direction. The inclined turntable also enables the ride to run with multiple different cycle paths, allowing the ride and hence ride time to vary. The ride features 12 airtime moments and a drive tyre lift hill.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride has two trains themed to off-road vehicles, each train has 10 cars, each seating 2 riders, for a total of 20 riders per train.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Brown track with brown supports. Trains are primarily green.

fullText:
Jungle Rush is a steel roller coaster at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia. It was built by Vekoma as the world's first Switchback rollercoaster. Jungle rush opened to the public on December 23, 2024.

Jungle Rush features an inclined turntable, which allows the train to change direction. The inclined turntable also enables the ride to run with multiple different cycle paths, allowing the ride and hence ride time to vary. The ride features 12 airtime moments and a drive tyre lift hill.

The ride has two trains themed to off-road vehicles, each train has 10 cars, each seating 2 riders, for a total of 20 riders per train.

Brown track with brown supports. Trains are primarily green.
